Saw the movie. It was very entertaining, but not extremely satisfying like I would hope a psychological horror would be.
This trailer twists some ideas, but they're very minor and forgivable.
 The ending was... confusing for me, but that's usually expected with these types of films.
7/10, definitely go see.

textDisplay: AMAZING MOVIE. Thought it would be good but never thought it would be THIS good. There were some parts that had bad acting but overall the movie was FANTASTIC. The scenes were so immersive and well done, everything was full of suspense and the movie itself was so immersive. It left me feeling all kinds of emotion for the characters. I can't explain. I applaud everyone on the team for making this movie. Me and my friends definitely enjoyed it!

I applaud everyone on the team for making this movie. Me and my friends definitely enjoyed it!

textDisplay: The trailer says win a million dollars but in the movie it's 10 grand. What's the reasoning for changing it to one million dollars for the ad?
textOriginal: The trailer says win a million dollars but in the movie it's 10 grand. What's the reasoning for changing it to one million dollars for the ad?

textDisplay: Albert DeSantis I think it’s because a million dollars would give away the morbid nature of the rooms. If it’s just 10K, the players will just think it’s a low stakes game and easy exercise.
textOriginal: Albert DeSantis I think it’s because a million dollars would give away the morbid nature of the rooms. If it’s just 10K, the players will just think it’s a low stakes game and easy exercise.

textDisplay: My friend and I came up with an idea for a story called TER (The Escape Rooms) more than a year before this trailer came out. Literally the one thing that’s different is the reason they came to the escape room and the amount of people. Welp, guess we have to trash our book now..? At least this movie looks really good. (Also it was really kinda creepy to see a trailer with the same plot and almost the same name it’s like we predicted the future.)
textOriginal: My friend and I came up with an idea for a story called TER (The Escape Rooms) more than a year before this trailer came out. Literally the one thing that’s different is the reason they came to the escape room and the amount of people. Welp, guess we have to trash our book now..? At least this movie looks really good. (Also it was really kinda creepy to see a trailer with the same plot and almost the same name it’s like we predicted the future.)

textDisplay: Ecopia You didn’t predict the future, your idea was just extremely unoriginal. There have been many movies like this, many of which inspired escape rooms. You basically ripped off an idea from a ripped off idea.
textOriginal: Ecopia You didn’t predict the future, your idea was just extremely unoriginal. There have been many movies like this, many of which inspired escape rooms. You basically ripped off an idea from a ripped off idea.

textDisplay: Video Guy true, but what movie or story plots haven’t been already thought of by others? It was just odd to see something so extremely similar come out a little more than a year later. textOriginal: Video Guy true, but what movie or story plots haven’t been already thought of by others? It was just odd to see something so extremely similar come out a little more than a year later. textDisplay: It’s sony. It’s going to be bad. If it’s not than Sony actually did something right for once, but as it stands Sony pictures is a huge dumpster fire. Kind of like welcome to the game In a way, but now that I’ve said that it’s not going to be surprising if that’s what it turns out to be. It’s gonna be the first thing I expect and I’m certain other people are thinking the same thing.

textDisplay:
This looks like it will be a pretty good movie. But I must say, the "death trap" movie concept, even if you're specifically looking at escape room movies, isn't exactly a novel idea lol. Funny enough, all except for two of them, plus the new one, are from Lionsgate.

Cube series (1997, 2002, 2004; Lionsgate) - same general idea, but not an "escape room," per se.
Saw franchise (2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2017; Lionsgate) - again, not an actual escape room, but it's still pretty much the same escape-or-die concept.
Cabin in the Woods (2012; Lionsgate) - not an escape room, but again close in concept to one.
Escape Room (2017; Global Genesis Group) - an escape room, but with a demonic killer inside.
Escape Room (2017; Lionsgate) - yes, there were two movies titled Escape Room that were released in 2017. This one is more on par with the new one coming out, a Saw-inspired escape room death trap.
No Escape Room (2018; MarVista Entertainment) - a SyFy movie about an escape room, but with an evil spirit inside.

textDisplay: Everyone saying this movie looks like Saw or The Cube, and I see where they're coming from, but at the same time this is the first movie I've ever seen marketed as an "escape room horror movie".  The characters willingly go to this location to do an escape room.  They know what they're getting themselves into (besides the part where they have to not die).  Escape rooms have become very popular in the past few years and Sony is just hopping on the train.  I honestly think this will be a really good movie whether or not you think it's a Saw/The Cube ripoff (which I personally do not)
textOriginal: Everyone saying this movie looks like Saw or The Cube, and I see where they're coming from, but at the same time this is the first movie I've ever seen marketed as an "escape room horror movie".  The characters willingly go to this location to do an escape room.  They know what they're getting themselves into (besides the part where they have to not die).  Escape rooms have become very popular in the past few years and Sony is just hopping on the train.  I honestly think this will be a really good movie whether or not you think it's a Saw/The Cube ripoff (which I personally do not)

textDisplay: Its Like the Movie Cube, With Modern Twist of Saw. Cool!

textDisplay:
Let me see 
1. Complex traps and rooms that are related to each person and have ironic deaths related to their past crimes or faults.
2. Being watched through video surveillance by the insane game maker. 
3. The insane game maker communicating to his victims through an audio device. 
4. At least one victim is killed in every room.
5. Clues are littered throughout each room to help the victims escape from set room
2 M. Night Shyamalan twists here:
           1. The insane game maker will meet the lone survivor at the end of the game,  and congratulate them on having a better appreciation for life. 
         2. The victims willingly go into their traps by thinking it was a simple escape room.
textOriginal:
Let me see 
1. Complex traps and rooms that are related to each person and have ironic deaths related to their past crimes or faults.
2. Being watched through video surveillance by the insane game maker. 
3. The insane game maker communicating to his victims through an audio device. 
4. At least one victim is killed in every room.
5. Clues are littered throughout each room to help the victims escape from set room
2 M. Night Shyamalan twists here:
           1. The insane game maker will meet the lone survivor at the end of the game,  and congratulate them on having a better appreciation for life. 
         2. The victims willingly go into their traps by thinking it was a simple escape room.
